epics.title: User Authentication
epics.summary: Implement secure user authentication using multi-factor authentication.
epics.description: Implement secure user authentication allowing customers to log in using multi-factor authentication methods, ensuring security and compliance with banking regulations. The authentication system should integrate with existing identity providers and support various forms of multi-factor authentication such as SMS, email, and authenticator apps. Tech stack: React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Database Schema Design
epics.summary: Design the database schema to securely store user data and application states.
epics.description: Design and implement the PostgreSQL database schema to securely store user data, application states, and interaction history. Ensure database security measures are in place, including encryption at rest and in transit. The schema should be optimized for performance and reliability. Tech stack: PostgreSQL.

epics.title: Core UI Development
epics.summary: Develop the core user interface components for the self-service portal.
epics.description: Develop the core user interface components for the banking self-service portal using React. Ensure the UI is responsive and provides a consistent experience across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices. The core UI should include the login screen, user dashboard, and basic navigation elements. Tech stack: React.

epics.title: Personalized Dashboard
epics.summary: Create a personalized dashboard displaying banking products and services based on user profile.
epics.description: Create a personalized dashboard that displays relevant banking products and services based on the user's profile and eligibility. The dashboard should provide an intuitive and user-friendly experience, allowing users to easily navigate through available options. Tech stack: React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Account Opening Workflow
epics.summary: Implement streamlined workflows for new account opening requests.
epics.description: Implement streamlined workflows allowing users to submit new account opening requests with minimal clicks. The system should guide users through the process, validate inputs, and ensure all necessary information and documentation are collected. Tech stack: React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Service Modifications Workflow
epics.summary: Enable users to submit service modification requests through streamlined workflows.
epics.description: Enable users to submit requests for modifications to their existing services through streamlined workflows. The system should allow users to select the service, provide necessary details, and upload any required documentation. Tech stack: React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Notification System
epics.summary: Implement real-time status updates and email notifications for user requests.
epics.description: Implement a notification system that provides real-time status updates and email notifications to users regarding their submitted requests. The system should ensure timely delivery and accurate status tracking. Tech stack: React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Integration with Core Banking System
epics.summary: Integrate the self-service portal with the bank's existing core banking system.
epics.description: Integrate the self-service portal with the bank's existing core banking system to ensure seamless data exchange and real-time synchronization of user activities and financial transactions. Tech stack: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Interaction History
epics.summary: Maintain a comprehensive history of all user interactions.
epics.description: Maintain a comprehensive history of all user interactions within the self-service portal, ensuring that users have a record of their activities and submitted requests. The interaction history should be securely stored and easily accessible by the user. Tech stack: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

epics.title: Role-Based Access Controls
epics.summary: Implement role-based access controls to manage user permissions.
epics.description: Implement role-based access controls to manage user permissions within the self-service portal. Ensure that users can only view and access services appropriate to their profile and authorization level, providing an added layer of security and compliance. Tech stack: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database.

user_stories.epic_title: Implement secure user authentication using multi-factor authentication.
user_stories.title: Setup Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
user_stories.description:
As a customer, I want to set up multi-factor authentication on my account, so that I can add an extra layer of security.

Core Domain Objects:
- OTP
- Token

Attributes & Rules:
- Must allow setup via SMS, email, and authenticator apps
- Must verify setup through a confirmation step
- Must allow reconfiguration of MFA methods

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Set up MFA via SMS
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects SMS as the MFA method
And enters their phone number
Then the system should send an OTP to the entered phone number
And the customer should confirm the OTP to complete the setup

Scenario: Set up MFA via email
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects email as the MFA method
And enters their email address
Then the system should send an OTP to the entered email address
And the customer should confirm the OTP to complete the setup

Scenario: Set up MFA via authenticator app
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects authenticator app as the MFA method
And scans the QR code in the app
Then the system should verify the setup via an authenticator-generated code

Test Cases:

Feature: MFA Setup

Scenario: Set up MFA via SMS
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects SMS as the MFA method
And enters their phone number
Then the system should send an OTP to the entered phone number
And the customer should confirm the OTP to complete the setup

Scenario: Set up MFA via email
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects email as the MFA method
And enters their email address
Then the system should send an OTP to the entered email address
And the customer should confirm the OTP to complete the setup

Scenario: Set up MFA via authenticator app
Given a customer is logged in
When the customer selects authenticator app as the MFA method
And scans the QR code in the app
Then the system should verify the setup via an authenticator-generated code

Priority: High - Critical for securing user accounts

user_stories.epic_title: Implement secure user authentication using multi-factor authentication.
user_stories.title: Implement SMS verification for MFA
user_stories.description:
As a user, I want to verify my identity using SMS as an authentication factor, so that I can add an additional layer of security to my account.

Core Domain Objects:
- OTP
- Token

Attributes & Rules:
- OTP must be sent to user's phone number
- OTP must expire in 5 minutes
- Retry attempts must be limited to 3

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Receive OTP via SMS
Given a user has chosen SMS for MFA
When the system sends an OTP to the user's phone number
Then the user should receive an OTP on their phone

Scenario: OTP expiration
Given a user has received an OTP
When the OTP exceeds its expiry time
Then the OTP should become invalid

Scenario: OTP retry limit
Given a user has failed to enter the OTP correctly
When the user attempts to retry
Then the system should allow retries up to 3 times only

Test Cases:

Feature: SMS Verification

Scenario: Receive OTP via SMS
Given a user has chosen SMS for MFA
When the system sends an OTP to the user's phone number
Then the user should receive an OTP on their phone

Scenario: OTP expiration
Given a user has received an OTP
When the OTP exceeds its expiry time
Then the OTP should become invalid

Scenario: OTP retry limit
Given a user has failed to enter the OTP correctly
When the user attempts to retry
Then the system should allow retries up to 3 times only

Priority: Medium - Enhances security

user_stories.title: Implement authenticator app verification for MFA
user_stories.description:
As a user, I want to verify my identity using an authenticator app, so that I can add an additional layer of security to my account.

Core Domain Objects:
- OTP
- Token

Attributes & Rules:
- Authenticator app must generate a time-based OTP
- OTP must expire in 30 seconds
- System must verify the OTP against the authenticator app's generated code

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Receive OTP via authenticator app
Given a user has chosen authenticator app for MFA
When the user opens the authenticator app
And retrieves the OTP
Then the user should enter the OTP in the system
And the system should verify the OTP

Scenario: Authenticator app OTP expiration
Given a user has generated an OTP using the authenticator app
When the OTP exceeds its expiry time of 30 seconds
Then the OTP should become invalid

Test Cases:

Feature: Authenticator App Verification

Scenario: Receive OTP via authenticator app
Given a user has chosen authenticator app for MFA
When the user opens the authenticator app
And retrieves the OTP
Then the user should enter the OTP in the system
And the system should verify the OTP

Scenario: Authenticator app OTP expiration
Given a user has generated an OTP using the authenticator app
When the OTP exceeds its expiry time of 30 seconds
Then the OTP should become invalid

Priority: Medium - Enhances security

user_stories.epic_title: Design the database schema to securely store user data and application states.
user_stories.title: Schema Design
user_stories.description:
As a database administrator, I want to design the database schema to securely store user data and application states so that the data is reliably structured and accessible.

Core Domain Objects:
- data
- schema
- table
- index

Attributes & Rules:
- schema validation
- performance optimization during schema design
- appropriate indexing for query performance

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Successful schema design
Given a set of requirements for the database
When I design the schema
Then the schema should be validated against the requirements
And optimize for performance

Scenario: Schema design failure due to invalid structure
Given a set of invalid requirements
When I attempt to design the schema
Then the validation should fail
And should provide error messages indicating the issues

Test Cases:

Feature: Schema Design

Scenario: Successful schema design
Given a set of requirements for the database
When I design the schema
Then the schema should be validated against the requirements
And optimize for performance

Scenario: Schema design failure due to invalid structure
Given a set of invalid requirements
When I attempt to design the schema
Then the validation should fail
And should provide error messages indicating the issues

Priority: High - Ensuring the database schema is correctly designed is fundamental to the application's data integrity and performance.

user_stories.epic_title: Design the database schema to securely store user data and application states.
user_stories.title: Data Encryption at Rest
user_stories.description:
As a developer, I want to implement encryption at rest for the database so that user data is protected from unauthorized access even if the storage medium is compromised.

Core Domain Objects:
- data
- encryption
- storage

Attributes & Rules:
- encryption at rest
- encryption standards (e.g. AES-256)
- key management

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Data encryption at rest
Given the database storage
When data is stored
Then it should be encrypted using AES-256
And encryption keys should be managed securely

Scenario: Decryption of encrypted data
Given encrypted data
When it is accessed by authorized personnel
Then it should be decrypted successfully

Test Cases:

Feature: Data Encryption at Rest

Scenario: Data encryption at rest
Given the database storage
When data is stored
Then it should be encrypted using AES-256
And encryption keys should be managed securely

Scenario: Decryption of encrypted data
Given encrypted data
When it is accessed by authorized personnel
Then it should be decrypted successfully

Priority: High - Protecting user data at rest helps in compliance and security measures.

user_stories.epic_title: Design the database schema to securely store user data and application states.
user_stories.title: Data Encryption in Transit
user_stories.description:
As a developer, I want to ensure data is encrypted in transit so that user data remains secure during data transfer between the application and the database.

Core Domain Objects:
- data
- encryption
- transit

Attributes & Rules:
- encryption in transit
- protocols (e.g. TLS)

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Data encryption in transit
Given the data transfer between the application and the database
When data is transmitted
Then it should be encrypted using TLS protocol

Scenario: Decryption during data receipt
Given encrypted data in transit
When it is received by the database or application
Then it should be decrypted successfully

Test Cases:

Feature: Data Encryption in Transit

Scenario: Data encryption in transit
Given the data transfer between the application and the database
When data is transmitted
Then it should be encrypted using TLS protocol

Scenario: Decryption during data receipt
Given encrypted data in transit
When it is received by the database or application
Then it should be decrypted successfully

Priority: High - Encrypting data in transit prevents interception and data breaches.

Figma Design Reference: https://www.figma.com/design/iwPK7TaEJfw92DWcFa7Sqc/banking---sdlc?node-id=1-5&m=dev

Core Domain Objects:
- Content Section
- Dynamic Elements

Attributes & Rules:
- Visibility
- Content Refresh

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Content display
Given the user
When accessing main content
Then dynamic content is loaded

Scenario: Content refresh
Given user
When interacting with main content
Then updates are visible

Scenario: Section responsiveness
Given user
When using main content area
Then interaction is smooth

Test Cases:

Feature: Main Content Area

Priority: Medium - Enhances user engagement

user_stories.title: Dashboard
user_stories.description:
As a user, I want a dashboard that integrates header, side nav, and main content, so that I can view all relevant data effectively.

Figma Design Reference - Header: https://www.figma.com/design/iwPK7TaEJfw92DWcFa7Sqc/banking---sdlc?node-id=1-280&m=dev
Figma Design Reference - Side Navigation Bar: https://www.figma.com/design/iwPK7TaEJfw92DWcFa7Sqc/banking---sdlc?node-id=1-224&m=dev
Figma Design Reference - Main Content Area: https://www.figma.com/design/iwPK7TaEJfw92DWcFa7Sqc/banking---sdlc?node-id=1-5&m=dev

Core Domain Objects:
- Dashboard Section
- Integrated Elements

Attributes & Rules:
- Visibility
- Interaction

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Dashboard visibility
Given the user
When accessing the dashboard
Then all integrated elements are visible

Scenario: Looking for key data
Given user
When using dashboard
Then critical data is accessible

Scenario: Dashboard responsiveness
Given user
When interacting with dashboard
Then it loads smoothly
